summary: Netherlandish Renaissance painter. Known for the Arnolfini Portrait, Ghent Altarpiece (with brother Hubert), and Man in a Red Turban. Vasari claimed he invented oil painting. Signature motto 'Als Ich Kan' ('As I can') puns on his name. Likely 'Hand G' of the Turin-Milan Hours.

works.name: Arnolfini Portrait (1434)
works.description: Double portrait often interpreted as depicting a wedding. Features artist's signature ('Jan van Eyck was here') on a wall, a convex mirror with Stations of the Cross on its frame, and a hand hold that may represent fides. Wikipedia

clues.clue: 1434 double portrait often interpreted as a wedding; artist's signature 'Jan van Eyck was here' on a wall near a convex mirror with Stations of the Cross on the frame
examples: the words 'was here,' as in a painting rendered on a wall in lavish calligraphy near the date '1434' and above a convex mirror whose wooden frame depicts the Stations of the Cross

clues.clue: The word 'AGLA' appears on floor tiles (also appears in the Archangel Michael buckler in another painting)
examples: the mysterious words 'Adoravi tetgrammathon agla' which also appears on floor tiles in another painting

works.name: Ghent Altarpiece (with Hubert van Eyck)
works.description: Polyptych created with brother Hubert. Bottom panel shows the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb. Includes St. Cecilia playing organ, God/Mary/John the Baptist in center, the Just Judges panel, and a nude Eve. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Polyptych with Adoration of the Mystic Lamb in bottom panel; St. Cecilia playing organ; God/Mary/John the Baptist in center; Just Judges panel shows both brothers as horsemen

clues.clue: Inscriptions say 'music in praise of God'; lettering curves around a seated God the Father
examples: Words meaning 'music in praise of God' accompany the depiction of angels playing music

comprehensive_summary:
Jan van Eyck (c. 1390-1441) was a Netherlandish Renaissance painter credited by Vasari with inventing oil painting (along with brothers Lambert and Hubert). His most commonly clued work is the Arnolfini Portrait (1434), a double portrait often interpreted as depicting a wedding, which features the artist's signature ('Jan van Eyck was here') written on a wall in lavish calligraphy near the date 1434, above a convex mirror whose wooden frame depicts the Stations of the Cross.

The Ghent Altarpiece, created with his brother Hubert, is a massive polyptych featuring the Adoration of the Mystic Lamb in the bottom panel, St. Cecilia playing the organ, God/Mary/John the Baptist in the center, and the famous Just Judges panel (which shows both brothers as horsemen). Inscriptions say 'music in praise of God.'

His signature motto 'Als Ich Kan' ('As I can') puns on his name. He is likely 'Hand G' who worked on the Turin-Milan Hours. Man in a Red Turban is considered a possible self-portrait. Leal Souvenir is a portrait that Erwin Panofsky claimed depicts the composer Gilles Binchois. His Annunciation features 'ecce ancilla Domini' written upside-down and a Book of Wisdom quote on Mary's robe.
